description: This figure is a CALPHAD-modelled phase amount versus temperature diagram for Fe40Mn19Ni15Al15Si10C1 high entropy steel. The x-axis represents temperature ranging from ~500 K to 1500 K, while the y-axis denotes the molar amount of all phases. Six curves correspond to distinct phases: 1) B2_VACANCY(AL:NI) (B2 ordered phase, ~0.28 mol at low temperature, gradually decreasing as temperature rises); 2) BCC_A2(FE,MN:VA) (dominant phase, maintaining 0.6-0.75 mol between 600 K and ~1100 K, then decreasing to nearly 0 at 1500 K); 3) BCC_A2#2(MN:VA,C) (trace phase with content <0.05 mol, stable up to ~1100 K); 4) LIQUID(FE,MN) (initiates formation at ~1100 K, rising to 1 mol at 1500 K); 5) M3SI(MN,FE:SI) (silicide phase, ~0.1 mol at low temperature, disappearing above ~750 K); 6) BCC_A2#3(FE:VA) (~0.45 mol below 600 K, fully vanishing at ~600 K). The diagram predicts the alloy is dominated by BCC_A2 and B2 phases below 1100 K with minor secondary phases, and fully melts at 1500 K, providing thermodynamic reference for experimental phase evolution analysis of the high entropy steel.
